sections.paragraph:
(Aram. כְּדִי, ܟܰܕ) pr. as who, as one who, Job 29:25 according to the different significations of each particle, it means-

(1) according to (that) which, hence-(a) according as, as much as. Gen 34:12, “and I will give כַּאֲשֶׁר תּאֹמְרוּ אֵלַי as much as ye shall ask from me;” 1Sa 2:16.-(b) according to what manner, i.q. as, like as, i.q. בְּ A. 2, but not prefixed to single words, but only to sentences. Gen 7:9, כַּאֲשֶׁר צִוָּה אֹתֹו אֱלֹהִים “as God had commanded him;” Gen 34:12 Isa 9:2 1Ki 8:57, also before an imperfect sentence ( Jos 8:6, “they flee כַּאֲשֶׁר בָּרִאשֹׁנָה as they fled formerly”);-(c) because (demgemäß daß). Num 27:14, כַּאֲשֶׁר מְרִיתֶם פִּי “because ye have rebelled against me;” 1Sa 28:18 2Ki 17:26 Mic 3:4. The use of the prefix כְּ in such phrases as כְּצִדְקִי corresponds with this. Very often כַּאֲשֶׁר … בֵּז answer to each other, as … so, Num 2:17 Isa 31:4 even as … so, Jud 1:7 in what degree … in the same, Exo 1:12 Isa 52:14 where there is a double protasis כַּאֲשֶׁר … כַּאֲשֶׁר … בֵּן, Isa 10:10, 11 Isa 10:11.

(2) as if, as though (wie wenn, compare אֲשֶׁר Conj. No. 4); Job 10:19, כַּאֲשֶׁר לֹא הָיִיתִי אֶהְיֶה “I should be as though I had not been;” Zec 10:6.

(3) as, so as, of time, i.q. כְּ prefixed to infinitives, Germ. wie, fowie, als, followed by a pret. i.q. after that, when. Exo 32:19, וַיְהִי כַּאֲשֶׁר קָרַב “and when he drew near to the camp.” Gen 29:10 1Sa 8:6 Exo 17:11 Est 4:16, כַּאֲשֶׁר אָבַדְתִּי אָבָ֑דְתִּי “when I shall perish, I perish,” wenn ich zu Grunde gegangen bin, fo bin ich es, the cry of one despairing; Gen 43:14. Followed by a fut. it has a conditional power. Ecc. 4:17 , “when (if) thou shalt go to the house of God.” 5:3 , “when thou shalt vow a vow.”
